questions.text: In Catalist, the product lists can be set by:
CMIR is set up for a specific Customer/ Customer Hierarchy and a specific L'Oréal EAN

options: All listings created for that customer and its hierarchy
Exclusions for the customer and it's hierarchy

questions.text: With Dynamic Maintenance a new Sold-To will be automatically included in the corresponding Customer List
Customer Listings or Exclusions are a way to control which quantities of a product a customer can order.
What must happen after amending a catalog in Catalist before receiving an up-to-date report?
options: The user must export the previous report
The catalog must be approved by the Country Admin
A recalculation must be run (max. 4 hours wait)
A new SKU must be created

questions.text: The Catalog Report will include any exclusions for the customer and its hierarchy.
If a customer is not assigned to any catalog, their report section will be blank—even if they can still order from signatures within their sales area.
You can only view CMIR-related fields in the report if you select the report down to the L6/Sold-To/Customer Code level.
Why might Customer Material Code not appear in the report?
options: The customer is not part of any catalog
A Forced SKU is used, and it overrides the active SKU
The product is no longer manufactured
The SD status is not selected
